calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Feb-03-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Robert Peterson for Jerry Morino (Administrator).
Marianne Blakeslee for Veronica Morino.

Mr. Peterson presented oral argument to support fees stating that this was a difficult three to four year Estate, that two accountings had to be filed by them and that they had to reconstruct everything from scratch. Ms. Blakeslee stated that she has no objection to Mr. Peterson's fees. The First Account and Report of Administrator is taken under submission.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Barrientos. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jan-04-2017 Continued To Feb-01-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Britta Peterson for Jerry Morino (Petitioner).

Ms. Peterson explained that they could not come up with time sheets for the period requested and that they don't generally keep time records. Ms. Peterson also explained time spent, work performed and services provided with respect to the sale of the property, and stated that she will obtain time sheets from the previous Attorney. The second and final account and report of Administrator is continued to February 1, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for Ms. Peterson to provide supplemental information.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Not reported.

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (110 15th Ave, San Francisco, Ca 94118)
Off Calendar Nov-10-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Robert Peterson III for petitioner Jerry Morino.

The Court noted that the petition was for the sale of a 100&% interest in the real property located at 110 15th Avenue, San Francisco California. He stated that notice had been appropriately given, and that the opening bid of $1,875,060.00 was a qualifying bid. There were no overbidders. The sale was confirmed at $1,875,060.00 based on the terms and conditions as set forth in the report of sale filed October 11, 2016. The Court granted the Petition to Confirm Sale of Real Property (subject to review of the order and posting of bond). An additional bond in the amount of $1,781,307.00 is required. Mr. peterson informed the Court that the sale was expected to close within three weeks. The Court set a status hearing re posting of bond for November 29, 2016 at 9:00 am in Department 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. (Not reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Aug-29-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Robert Peterson for (administrator) Jerry Morino. Marian Blakeslee for Veronica Morino.

Mr. Peterson reported that he was not the attorney of record at the time the probate was filed, that his client has done everything he can to rectify, that his client has advanced funds, that in regards to the preliminary distribution it was approved by the heirs, that no harm has been done, that it was an informal distribution between siblings and that the property is now for sale. Ms. Blakeslee stated that her client is the surviving spouse, that they are not happy with the way things were handled, that under Mr. Peterson's representation they are doing the best they can, that her client does not wish to object and that the administrator has stated that he will not file for fees. The accounting petition was taken under submission.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Grimaldi. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Aug-01-2016 Continued To Aug-22-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Jul-25-2016 Continued To Aug-01-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Jun-20-2016 Continued To Nov-18-16 At 08:30 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Robert Peterson III for petitioner Jerry Morino; Marian Blakeslee for the estate of Frank Morino, Beneficiary.

Mr. Peterson reported that previously the beneficiaries had intended that two of them were going to buy out the thirs's interest in the real property, but that such was no longer the plan, and the property was being put up for sale. He stated that the property is being listed for sale, and the current tenant would be moving out of the premises in August. He said that he plans to have the accounting on file within two weeks. Ms. Blakeslee stated concern that the administrator has not met any of the statutory deadlines for performance of his duties, but agreed that as Mr. Peterson had only recently substitiuted into the case, two weeks for filing of the accounting was fair. Mr. Peterson added that while the property is already listed, there is a lot of personal proberty of the decedent which will need to be sold by conducting an estate sale, as removal of that personal property would make the real property more saleable. He stated that it was the listing realtor's belief that the estate sale should be conducted before showing the property for sale. In response to the Court's request for a timeline for closing the estate, Mr. Peterson stated his estimate as six months; Ms. Blakeslee opined that it could be concluded in four months. The Court set November 18, 2016 as the deadline for hearing a petition for close of the estate, and continued the status hearing to 9:00 am on that date in Dept. 204.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not reported)

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar Apr-07-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lauren Kusumoto for and with (petitioner) Jerry Morino. Marian Blakeslee for Veronica Morino, executor of the estate of Frank Morino.

The Court asked if it would not be more sensible to have the reappraisal, the accounting and then submit objections. Ms. Blakeslee stated that she would like to add that the missing link is whether the administrator could get financing. Ms. Kusomoto reported that they have contacted a lender, that financing is ready to go, that they just got the appraisers name and information and find that he is not available for 4 weeks, that it will be late May before they can do a further accounting and that there may be a need for a third appraisal. Ms. Blakeslee stated that if the appraisals are done close in time there should not be a problem, that if a third appraisal is necessary she would like to be included in selecting an appraiser and that they would like an accounting by May 15, 2016. The Court issued the following dates: reappraisals due May 9, 2016, accounting due May 19, 2016, financing to be in place by June 1, 2016 and set a status hearing date of June 20, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status on each of these items. The probate accounting status report is continued to that date also. Ms. Kusumoto is to prepare an order regarding the above dates.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)
